goal: Quick Pull should show the card's PRESENT interpretation as the answer (not the reflection question), and the poster card name shouldn't overlap the badge
root_cause: For a single Quick Pull card, both the poster summary resolver (resolveQuestionPosterSummaries) and the result-page inline used a fallback chain that reached reflection_question (a prompt, e.g. The Fool 'If you knew you wouldn't get hurt falling...') before any standalone present interpretation — so when a topic-specific present field was absent (generic/'other' topic, or a TH content gap) the reading showed a QUESTION instead of an answer, sometimes the EN question on a TH poster. Separately the poster card name (cardBottomY+132, up to 96px italic) overlapped the 'Answer · Upright' badge (cardBottomY+70) by ~17px.
fix: poster.js resolveQuestionPosterSummaries: inserted standalone_${slot} into the chain and REMOVED reflection_question entirely (it's a prompt, not an answer); added EN gap-fillers (standalone_${slot}_en, general_meaning_en, tarot_imply_en) so a TH content gap shows an English interpretation statement rather than a question or a blank (free-core-complete). renderQuickPullPoster: card name baseline 132->152 to clear the badge. reading.js renderQuestion single-card inline now leads with standalone_present (+EN gap-fill for TH), never the reflection question.
verified: Harness (chrome-devtools MCP, cache-busted, real card art): EN topic=love -> love_present, EN topic=other -> standalone_present ('Right now, life is inviting you to take a risk...') NOT the reflection question; TH love/other -> standalone_present_en gap-fill (statement, not question, not blank); card name 'The Fool' now clears the 'Answer · Upright' badge. Result-page inline change is logic-only (manual QA pending).
